suppress.notes: CPE false positive: dependency-check matches Google.Protobuf.dll 3.35.1 (the .NET NuGet package) against CVE-2026-0994, but the finding's own description is about google.protobuf.json_format.ParseDict() in the Python protobuf runtime (cpe:2.3:a:google:protobuf / cpe:2.3:a:protobuf:protobuf), not the C# implementation. Confirmed 2026-07-24: Google.Protobuf 3.35.1 is already the latest stable release on NuGet (only 4.0.0-rc1/rc2 prereleases exist above it), so there is no newer stable version to bump to even if the match were real.

CPE false positive: matches the .NET JsonPointer.Net library (gregsdennis/JsonPointer.Net, CPE cpe:2.3:a:json-pointer_project:json-pointer) against CVE-2022-4742, which is a prototype-pollution vulnerability in the unrelated Node.js npm package `json-pointer` (manuelstofer/json-pointer), fixed there in 0.6.2. The two projects share a name but are different languages/ecosystems/authors. Confirmed 2026-07-24: JsonPointer.Net 7.0.1 is already the latest version on NuGet.

Microsoft.NET.StringTools.dll is not a NuGet package this repo references directly — it ships bundled inside the .NET 10 SDK (part of MSBuild's toolchain, brought in transitively by the SDK/build tooling, not pinned in Directory.Packages.props). CVE-2025-26646 is a real Microsoft advisory (spoofing via external control of file/path), but it is fixed by updating the .NET SDK / Visual Studio / Build Tools installation used to build this repo, not by bumping a NuGet package reference — there is nothing in this project's dependency graph to change. Tracked as an environment/toolchain concern outside this repo's package management (see .specs/project/STATE.md D-003/G-004).

CPE false positive, all 13 CVEs: dependency-check matches Nerdbank.MessagePack.dll 1.2.36 (Andrew Arnott's NuGet package, cpe:2.3:a:messagepack:messagepack / cpe:2.3:a:messagepack_project:messagepack) against advisories whose own description text explicitly names "MessagePack for C#" fixed in 2.5.301/3.1.7 -- that is the separate MessagePack-CSharp project (neuecc/MessagePack-CSharp), a different NuGet package/author from Nerdbank.MessagePack. CVE-2022-41719's description references "Unmarshal" panics in github.com/shamaton/msgpack, a Go library, also unrelated. Confirmed 2026-07-24: Nerdbank.MessagePack 1.2.36 is the latest stable release on NuGet (the only version above it, 1.3.29-beta, is a prerelease not adopted for production use), so even if these findings were real there is no stable patched version to bump to.

CPE false positive: dependency-check matches the .NET OpenTelemetry/OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.AspNetCore NuGet packages (v1.17.0) against CVE-2026-39883, whose own description explicitly states "OpenTelemetry-Go is the Go implementation of OpenTelemetry" -- a PATH-hijacking bug in the Go SDK's BSD `kenv` command lookup, fixed there in v1.43.0. This is a different project (open-telemetry/opentelemetry-go) from the .NET open-telemetry/opentelemetry-dotnet packages referenced here. Confirmed 2026-07-24: OpenTelemetry 1.17.0 is already the latest stable release on NuGet, so there is no newer stable version to bump to even if the match were real. Covers both OpenTelemetry.dll and OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.AspNetCore.dll, which share this evidence.
